channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: China protests U.S. Treasury’s sanctions for hacking incidents
item.description: China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cybersecurity agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft will invest $80B in AI data centers in fiscal 2025
item.description: Microsoft is on track to invest around $80 billion this fiscal year to build out AI-enabled data centers, company president Brad Smith said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: This new robot vacuum-and-mop is so eagle-eyed it can even spy clear liquid spillages
item.description: The Eureka J15 Max Ultra uses AI to detect even the most subtlest differences in texture and reflections.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tesla data helped police after Las Vegas truck explosion, but experts have wider privacy concerns
item.description: Privacy data experts say the deep dive by Elon Musk’s company was impressive, but also shines a spotlight on a difficult question as vehicles become less like cars and more like computers on wheels.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: United Airlines rolling out Elon Musk's Starlink Wi-Fi this spring
item.description: United Airlines plans to launch its first commercial flight with Starlink Wi-Fi this spring after accelerating its timeline for the new internet service, the company announced Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Artificial intelligence tech firm will expand in San Jose with new HQ
item.description: An artificial intelligence and tech company will move its headquarters to north San Jose in an expansion.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Salt Typhoon attacks may have hit more US firms than previously thought
item.description: China denies involvement as more US telecoms providers are revealed to be victims of Salt Typhoon attack.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: What to know about the Meta glasses the New Orleans attacker used to scout the French Quarter
item.description: The FBI says the New Orleans truck attacker who killed 14 people had previously scouted the French Quarter and recorded video with his Meta smart glasses.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Missing Link: How Enterprise PMOs Drive Strategic Execution
item.description: Unlocking the full potential of a Project Management Office (PMO) to drive strategic execution and business outcomes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Elaine Kurtenbach, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: TikTok creators left in limbo while awaiting decision on potential platform ban
item.description: The fate of TikTok is keeping creators and small business owners in anxious limbo as they await a decision from the Supreme Court that could upheld their livelihoods.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Landrum Jr. And Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Create custom visuals on your iPhone with Image Playground in iOS 18.2
item.description: Apple's Image Playground transforms text prompts into images or animations in seconds, brining a world of creativity to the palm of your hand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOJ calls on Supreme Court to deny Trump's request to delay TikTok ban
item.description: The Justice Department urged the Supreme Court on Friday to deny President-elect Trump’s request to postpone their ruling on a TikTok ban set to be implemented the day before his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A double dose of nasty winter is about to smack much of the US with snow, ice and biting cold
item.description: Meteorologists forecast a strong snow and ice storm followed by brutally cold conditions will soon smack the eastern two-thirds of the United States.
item.dc:creator: By Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The vital role of red teaming in safeguarding AI systems and data
item.description: Companies are feeling pressure to adopt generative AI to stay ahead in a competitive global market. To ensure they adopt responsibly, governing and regulatory bodies across the world are convening to debate and understand the best ways to address AI risk without stifling their innovation edge.
item.dc:creator: Info World
